calendarNotes:
Broward County Public Schools begins the 2025-2026 school year on August 11, 2025 — among the earliest start dates in Florida and nationally. Spring Break runs March 16–20, 2025, and the school year ends June 5, 2025. In 2025-2026, Broward shares the same Spring Break window as neighboring Miami-Dade and Palm Beach County — an alignment that doesn't occur every year. Orange County Public Schools (Orlando) breaks a week earlier, March 15–19.

For families planning travel, the 2025-2026 alignment means all three South Florida counties release simultaneously on March 22 — making that week one of the most congested and expensive travel periods in the region. Flights, hotels, and theme parks across Florida peak that week. Families with flexibility should consider the prior week (March 15–19), when only Orlando-area schools are on break and South Florida prices have not yet spiked.

Broward County sits between Miami-Dade to the south and Palm Beach to the north, and a significant number of families live near county lines or have extended family in adjacent districts. The Spring Break alignment in 2025-2026 is convenient, but in other years it can diverge by a week — families coordinating across county lines should always re-verify each district's official calendar before booking. Miami-Dade's school year extends to June 3, while Broward's ends May 28, a difference that matters for families coordinating summer camps or childcare.

Broward County lies within the Atlantic hurricane zone, and Florida law permits districts to bank up to three emergency makeup days into the calendar. If closures from a named storm exceed this buffer, the district may extend the school year beyond the planned May 28 end date. Families booking June travel from the Fort Lauderdale area should use flexible cancellation options if the district has had significant weather disruptions during the school year. Peak hurricane risk for South Florida runs August through October — directly overlapping with the start of the school year.

BCPS operates a robust magnet school and school choice system, with specialized programs in STEM, performing arts, aviation, and International Baccalaureate at multiple grade levels. The district's school choice application process opens in October for the following school year, with most programs closing applications by December or January. Families interested in specialty programs should attend a fall choice fair and apply early — STEM and IB programs at the high school level are particularly competitive.

Port Everglades in Fort Lauderdale is one of the busiest cruise ports in the world, and many Broward families time vacations around the school calendar by booking cruises that depart the day the school year ends. Late May is one of the most active embarkation periods at Port Everglades — 7-night sailings departing the weekend after Broward's May 28 last day are typically sold out or at peak pricing by late March. Families planning a post-school-year cruise should book by February at the latest. Spring break sailings (March 16–20) are also popular but compete with families from all three South Florida counties releasing simultaneously that week.
